_risk_level_doc: medium — security-relevant fix to permission-grant matcher (D) and a workflow-validator state-tracking fix (H). Bounded blast radius: D touches one library function used by 6 production callers all with exact tool names; H changes legacy Path B of a single posttool hook with explicit fall-back to existing behavior, gated by mutually-exclusive Case A / Case B chain.

requirement.clarified: Land code-fix items D + H from the prior Codex triage of leftover work after cycle 20260519-151734 closed. D: tighten PreTool /allow literal match to exact-only to mirror PostTool consumption semantics, closing a permission-grant leakage. H: replace bookmark-in_progress inference in posttool-subagent-track.py with deterministic canonical-step lookup keyed on the dispatched Agent subagent_type (bounded-window K=3 first-unflipped-match), closing a race when TodoWrite + Agent dispatch in one orchestrator response. Item A (live reproduction of read-size-guard /do bypass) is handled by the orchestrator post-cycle and is NOT a BA/dev deliverable.
requirement.what: Patch hooks/lib/allowlist.py (one enum branch + one call-site policy change + docstring) and hooks/posttool-subagent-track.py (add deterministic bounded-window resolution helper, reorder _main resolution chain into mutually-exclusive Case A / Case B, mandate None-key guard before writer). Add regression test for D.
requirement.why: Two pre-existing harness defects observed during cycle 20260519-151734: a permission-grant leakage vector and a workflow-deadlock-on-parallel-dispatch race. The user directive is '不放松任何权限' (no permission loosening); both fixes either tighten or preserve existing semantics — no loosening.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: D: User types /allow Re; Read tool call passes the PreTool /allow bypass; PostTool fails to consume the grant; grant file persists past the single-use contract documented in userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh:12 and the lib/allowlist.py docstring at line 240-241. H: When TodoWrite and Agent dispatch in a single orchestrator response, PostToolUse:Agent reads the bookmark before posttool-todo-sequence.py writes the new last_todos; tracker sees stale in_progress (N-1), exits early without flipping subagent_calls; subsequent TodoWrite (Step N → completed) is blocked by Gate 4 in pretool-todo-validate.py.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: D: PreTool read_grant passes literal_policy='exact_or_substr' to _match_loaded_grant (hooks/lib/allowlist.py:139), while PostTool consume_grant_for_posttool branch 3 hard-codes exact equality (hooks/lib/allowlist.py:291-292). The two paths use different literal-match semantics for the same grant file. H: posttool-subagent-track.py legacy Path B (_resolve_context line 340-358, _main line 370-389) infers which canonical step to flip from the bookmark's in_progress flag. When TodoWrite and Agent dispatch in a single response, the bookmark's in_progress flag has not yet been updated by posttool-todo-sequence.py when PostToolUse:Agent fires, causing the tracker to look at the wrong (stale) canonical index.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: D: aaf3c44 (refactor extracting lib/allowlist.py — introduced exact_or_substr policy as the OR-of-existing-behaviors during refactor; the PostTool path retained exact-only as a defensive measure documented in branch 3 docstring). H: efca767 (feat: add Gate 4 subagent_call enforcement to workflow system — introduced the strict validator without designing for parallel TodoWrite+Agent dispatch).
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: D: During the allow-6 consolidation (task 20260518-155948), _match_loaded_grant was extracted from multiple call sites and the literal_policy enum was introduced to capture the divergent existing behaviors (some callers needed substring, some needed exact). The PreTool read_grant call was wired with 'exact_or_substr' as a superset that would not break any prior caller, while PostTool consumption retained its narrower exact-only logic. H: Gate 4 was added with the assumption that orchestrator dispatch is sequential — TodoWrite first to commit in_progress, then Agent in a subsequent response. The single-response dual-dispatch pattern emerged later and was not in the original design's threat model.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: D: The mismatch produces a permission-grant leakage vector. A user typing /allow Re could (intentionally or by typo) issue a grant that PreTool honors as a single-use bypass for Read but PostTool refuses to consume. The grant then persists for ALL subsequent Read tool calls until the next Stop hook cleanup, effectively converting a single-use grant into a session-wide bypass. H: The race makes the harness deadlock on legitimate parallel dispatch patterns. A skilled orchestrator emitting TodoWrite + Agent in one response (a faster, more efficient pattern than serial dispatch) trips Gate 4 and requires manual bookmark patching to recover.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: D: Asymmetry has existed since the allow-6 consolidation landed (aaf3c44, before this branch's current dirty changes). H: Race has existed since Gate 4 was introduced (efca767); first reproduced operationally during cycle 20260519-151734.

pre_existing_guards.purpose: Exact-only match on PostTool consumption side; explicitly named in docstring as the prevention of cross-tool consumption. This is the SOURCE of the asymmetry we are aligning PreTool to.

Gate 4 strictness — completing a subagent_call step requires the dispatch to have been recorded. The H fix decouples WHEN the dispatch is recorded from WHEN the bookmark's in_progress flips, but MUST NOT weaken this check.

Safety early-exit when the in_progress step lacks subagent_call metadata. Under the H fix, the resolution path tries subagent_type-keyed lookup FIRST (Case A); this early-exit is preserved in the FALLBACK path (Case B) as defense-in-depth.

evidence.observed: D: Codex triage flagged PreTool/PostTool literal-match asymmetry as a permission-grant leakage vector (severity=high, effort=small, passes Section 5.4 rule 2 security exception). measured.value: D: PreTool literal_policy=exact_or_substr (line 139) vs PostTool exact-only (lines 291-292); same grant file, different match. H: Tracker step resolution at _resolve_context line 354 reads bookmark.in_progress; bookmark is stale on parallel dispatch.

expected.value: D: PreTool literal match aligns to exact-only (mirrors PostTool semantics, tightens permission). H: Tracker resolves target canonical index by dispatched subagent_type via BOUNDED-WINDOW first-unflipped-match (K=3): scans canonical[max(0,ip_index):ip_index+3]; on miss, exits 0 with no write (no fall-through). Case B (subagent_type absent) runs the existing legacy chain as a separate path; on None resolution there, also exits 0 with no write. None-key guard mandates target is not None and isinstance(target, int) before the writer.
expected.source: D: hooks/lib/allowlist.py:240-241 (consume_grant_for_posttool docstring — design contract). H: hooks/pretool-subagent-enforce.py:128 (field-shape evidence that tool_input.subagent_type IS the canonical dispatch channel); hooks/pretool-todo-validate.py:227-241 (Gate 4 strictness contract the tracker must preserve). Bounded-window K=3 chosen per OBJ-3 path (a) resolution (Codex preferred direction): admits the common parallel-dispatch race + one-step-ahead defense margin, but prevents reaching stale steps.

evidence.gap: D: One policy enum branch addition + one string change at the call site. No removal of existing guards; no API signature change. None-key write hazard explicitly closed by pre-writer type guard.

development_approach.strategy: D: align PreTool literal match to PostTool exact-only semantics by adding a third literal_policy enum value 'exact_only' to _match_loaded_grant and switching read_grant's call to use it. H: replace bookmark in_progress inference with deterministic canonical-step lookup keyed on stdin.tool_input.subagent_type with BOUNDED-WINDOW K=3 first-unflipped-match (option (b), OBJ-3 path (a) resolution). Mutually-exclusive Case A (subagent_type non-empty → typed lookup → exit-0-no-write on miss; NO fall-through to legacy) / Case B (subagent_type absent → existing legacy chain → exit-0-no-write on None). None-key guard before _record_subagent_call_legacy.
development_approach.validation_approach: Static: pytest hooks/tests/test_allowlist_consolidation.py (existing + new test for AC-D1). Smoke: invoke each of the 6 production PreTool read_grant call sites once with exact pattern + once with substring pattern, assert exact returns True / substring returns False (AC-D3). For H: simulate parallel dispatch via direct Python test invocation of posttool-subagent-track.py with a synthesized bookmark and stdin payload — assert subagent_calls[<expected_idx>]=True is recorded via Case A bounded-window lookup (AC-H1); simulate serial dispatch — same assertion (AC-H2); simulate orphan Agent in Case A (subagent_type non-empty, no match within window) — assert no exception, no None key written, no wrong-step flip (AC-H3); simulate orphan Agent in Case B (subagent_type absent, legacy returns None) — assert no exception, no None key (AC-H3); leave pretool-todo-validate.py untouched and re-run any existing harness tests against it to confirm Gate 4 still blocks (AC-H4). Re-run the prior cycle's smoke for AC-D-H (read-size-guard + block-enterworktree /allow bypass) with /allow Read and /allow EnterWorktree to confirm exact match still works.

iteration_log.reason: QA BA-validation rejection of iter0 with 3 objections (2 BLOCKER + 1 MAJOR). All resolved by targeted text edits to the spec + JSON; no new investigation required.

objections_addressed.resolution: AC-D3 and the 'All occurrences of read_grant(' enumeration revised: '8 PreTool call sites' was imprecise — the count included 3 test invocations and 1 lib definition, neither of which is a call site. Iter1 re-labels the enumeration as '6 production callers + 1 test file with 3 calls + 1 lib definition' and rewrites AC-D3 to enumerate exactly the 6 production callers (orchestrator-gate.py:184, subagent-enforce.py:214, write-guard.sh:39, aggregate-check.py:389, read-size-guard.py:72, block-enterworktree.sh:48) with explicit line citations as the mechanically reproducible target list. Won't Have, Constraints, MoSCoW Must-Have D3, and the D-risk call-site-regression risk-section all updated to match. component_chain expanded to include each of the 6 production callers + the 2 non-caller occurrences (test file + lib def) with PRODUCTION-CALLER vs NOT-A-CALL-SITE labels.

H Technical Hints step 1 revised to split the resolution chain into two mutually-exclusive cases: Case A (subagent_type non-empty → call _find_earliest_unflipped_matching_step → on valid index, flip; on None, exit 0 with NO write; NO fall-through to legacy) and Case B (subagent_type absent → existing legacy _current_in_progress_index + _step_has_subagent_call chain → on None, exit 0 with NO write). This prevents both sub-defects: (i) None-key write hazard — closed by explicit None-key guard mandating `target is not None and isinstance(target, int)` before _record_subagent_call_legacy; (ii) cross-type wrong-step flip — closed by Case A's no-fall-through policy (when subagent_type='ba' but in_progress is qa, the typed lookup misses and the hook exits 0 with no write, preserving Gate 4 strictness for cross-type dispatches). AC-H3 tightened to explicitly assert no None key, no wrong-step flip, no fall-through corruption.

Adopted Codex's preferred direction (a): bound the typed lookup to window canonical[max(0, ip_index):ip_index+3] (K=3). _find_earliest_unflipped_matching_step now accepts ip_index as a parameter and scans only that bounded slice. K=3 rationale recorded in Edge Cases & Risks: admits the common parallel-dispatch race (ip_index itself, offset 0) plus a small one-step-ahead defense margin (covers orchestrators that batch Agent emissions slightly before the matching TodoWrite update), but small enough to prevent reaching back to stale-state steps that should already be considered complete. K>3 would admit stale-state misflips (e.g., session-restart with qa-1 already-completed-but-not-flipped accidentally matched by a new qa-2 intent); K<3 would miss legitimate parallel dispatches. Outside the window, the tracker exits 0 with no write (per Case A miss handling).

iteration_log.reason: QA iter2 BA-validation rejection with 3 new objections (2 BLOCKER + 1 MAJOR) on residual drift from iter1. After orchestrator promoted the D+H artifacts from -DH suffix to canonical paths at ~05:23 UTC, BA iter3 applied targeted text edits to the canonical files to resolve all 3 OBJ. No new investigation; pure spec/JSON text edits.

objections_addressed.resolution: AC-H3 split into AC-H3a (subagent_type ABSENT + legacy resolves a single valid in_progress index with subagent_call metadata → legacy write allowed; preserves AC-H2 serial-dispatch behavior) and AC-H3b (four enumerated no-write orphan sub-cases: (1) subagent_type absent + legacy returns None; (2) subagent_type non-empty + typed lookup miss; (3) subagent_type non-empty + no in_progress; (4) subagent_type non-empty + multi-in_progress). This removes the iter1 contradiction where AC-H3's general GIVEN listed 'missing subagent_type' as an orphan trigger while Technical Hints Case B allowed legacy write in that same state.

Helper _find_earliest_unflipped_matching_step rewritten: removed the iter1 `else: start = 0` fallback that opened canonical[0:3] when ip_index is None. New behavior: if ip_index is None, return None immediately — caller exits 0 no-write per AC-H3b sub-cases (3) and (4). Case A is now gated on a single valid in_progress anchor. Additionally, added a multi-in_progress guard requirement at _current_in_progress_index entry path: if more than one in_progress step is found, return None — bookmark is inconsistent, safer to no-write than to pick an arbitrary anchor. Concrete reference implementation embedded in Technical Hints H section. Edge Cases & Risks H section gained an explicit OBJ-5 bullet documenting the single-anchor gating rule.

Companion artifact paths reconciled with orchestrator's collision-resolution cleanup. acceptance_criteria_path set to null and blast_radius_map_path set to null — D+H scope (2 hooks + 1 test file; 9 ACs total) is too small to require companion JSONs; the ACs in ticket-20260519-211515.md are authoritative. Added _companion_artifacts_note documenting the rationale and pointing at the ghost Cycle 2 companion artifacts now at -CYCLE2 suffix. collision_report.preserved_artifact_paths[] updated to reflect the actual on-disk state: the D+H ticket+context were promoted to canonical at ~05:23 UTC by the orchestrator, and the ghost Cycle 2 AC+blast-radius companion artifacts were moved to -CYCLE2 suffix at ~06:00 UTC; orchestrator-side collision-resolution cleanup is complete for this cycle.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: iter1 codex invocation succeeded via the tee-less channel (the workaround the QA cycle discovered). Codex was asked to review BA's refusal-and-preserve strategy. Codex concurred: (1) contract_violation_refused is the right status, with a precise blocker field; (2) BA should NOT overlay D+H content onto parallel cycle's JSON — refuse and escalate; (3) write preserved D+H artifacts to renamed (-DH) paths as the least-destructive recovery option; (4) do NOT reapply D+H edits onto the now-parallel-cycle markdown — escalation is correct.

findings.issue: Status vocabulary precision: should BA use contract_violation_refused (existing) or a new task_id_collision_blocked?
findings.proposed_fix: Use contract_violation_refused with precise blocker field: blocker='task_id_collision', reason='canonical artifacts were overwritten by parallel cycle'. If new vocabulary is allowed, task_id_collision_blocked or artifact_overwritten_by_parallel_cycle is more precise.
findings.rationale: iter1 retains contract_violation_refused with the precise blocker field; orchestrator can elect to introduce new vocabulary in a future schema revision.

findings.issue: Should BA Edit-overlay onto parallel cycle's JSON?
findings.proposed_fix: NO. Refuse and escalate. Overlaying would knowingly destroy another cycle's work.
findings.rationale: BA explicitly refused. Pretool-write-guard.sh independently blocked the overwrite attempt.

findings.issue: Is there a third option preserving both cycles?
findings.proposed_fix: YES — write preserved D+H artifacts to renamed paths (docs/dev/ticket-20260519-211515-DH.md, docs/dev/context-20260519-211515-DH.json). Least-destructive recovery; orchestrator decides canonicality.
findings.rationale: BA applied this option in iter1: wrote both -DH artifacts non-destructively.

findings.issue: Should BA reapply D+H edits onto the now-parallel-cycle markdown?
findings.proposed_fix: NO. That would destroy the parallel cycle's markdown. Escalation is correct.
findings.rationale: BA respected the canonical path's new owner (parallel cycle) and preserved D+H content only under -DH suffix.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: iter1 codex consultation succeeded (status: ok). All recommendations applied: status=contract_violation_refused with blocker=task_id_collision; refuse-and-preserve strategy; -DH suffixed artifacts written non-destructively; escalation to orchestrator with explicit resolution_required field.

orchestrator_required_resolution: Decide which BA cycle owns the canonical 20260519-211515 artifact slot.
If the D+H cycle is canonical, rename *-DH.md / *-DH.json to the canonical paths after relocating the parallel cycle's artifacts to its own task-id.
If the parallel cycle is canonical, reassign the D+H cycle a fresh task-id and rename *-DH.* to that new id.
Diagnose how two BA cycles received the same task-id from the orchestrator's task-id allocator (likely a clock-second collision or a missing distinctness check) and patch the allocator.
